law: DriverKit on iPadOS requires an iPad with an M-series chip.
Use this app to install or update the driver, and then to call the driver directly.
You can set up the project to build with automatic signing (recommended) or manual signing.
To use automatic signing: Temporarily turn off SIP, as described in the article .
Select the project and use the “Signing & Capabilities” tab to set the and targets to “Automatically manage signing”.
Use a reverse-DNS format for your identifier, as described in .
In the Xcode Project navigator, choose the project and use the Signing & Capabilities tab to replace the existing bundle identifier with your chosen identifier for each of the targets.
Although this simplifies running the sample code, in your own apps you may prefer to use .
For the Bundle ID, choose “explicit”, and use the names you chose in the first step.
When creating the driver’s profile, be sure to select DriverKit App Development as the profile type.
